basics.name: Luis Enrique Torres Cervantes
basics.label: Jr Systems Engineer & Jr Web Developer
basics.summary: Junior Systems Engineer and full-stack developer focused on software development. Practical experience developing web modules integrated with enterprise SQL Server databases within corporate infrastructure, supporting internal business processes.

awards.title: Coding Cup Tecnm Apatzingan
awards.summary: Led team of 3 developers, solving 10 algorithmic challenges in 4 hours, competing against 3 teams from the Instituto Tecnologico Superior Apatzingan.

work.name: Process Automations
work.summary: Developed a web-based budget capture module integrated with an enterprise SQL Server database to standardize internal budgeting workflows, enabling structured data capture and version control for budget change requests and approvals.
highlights: Typescript, Angular 17, SQL Server, C#, ASP.NET Core

work.name: Web-based static 3D visualization module
work.summary: Developed a web-based static 3D visualization module for ArcelorMittal GO (AMGO) using Three.js. Displayed operational information from multiple areas of ArcelorMittal Mexico to support personnel orientation and route planning

projects.name: Pageper – browser extension
projects.description: Developed a minimalist, distraction-free homepage, published on the official Firefox extension store. Implemented dynamic data loading using JSON files and state management with React. Automated build and packaging workflows using Shell scripts to optimize testing and deployment.

projects.name: IoT-based irrigation system
projects.description: Developed an IoT-based irrigation system prototype using scheduled automation and real-time sensor data to control watering processes Aimed to reduce manual labor, Optimized water usage Provided a low-cost, customizable solution for greenhouse environments

projects.name: Delfin research summer
projects.description: Conducted a six-week research fellowship focused on Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) applications and real-time device manipulation using Machine Learning (Scikit-learn) Achieved 80% of accuracy on BCI predictions
